product.name: Tuxborn Wiki
product.tagline: A structured field guide for the Tuxborn Skyrim modpack.

hero.headline: The Tuxborn field atlas for install, playthrough planning, and troubleshooting.
hero.subhead: This site mirrors and organizes the Tuxborn GitHub Wiki so players can move quickly between setup guides, quest references, follower notes, and evolving FAQs.

summary: Tuxborn Wiki is a maintained reference layer for a large Skyrim modpack, with indexed gameplay topics, compatibility guidance, and checklist-driven progress tracking.
audience: Players installing Tuxborn for the first time
Returning players planning a new run
Steam Deck and handheld users managing profile and settings differences
coreProblems: Critical setup and profile choices are easy to miss in a large modpack
Quest and content routing can become fragmented across scattered notes
Support channels repeatedly receive the same foundational questions
Players need a practical way to track completion across broad content

features.title: Wiki-first publishing model
features.summary: Maintainers keep authoring in GitHub Wiki markdown while this site syncs and publishes a clean static mirror.

features.title: Topic-indexed navigation
features.summary: Major areas like Getting Started, Content and Quests, Followers, FAQ, and Modding are organized as navigable hubs.

features.title: Checklist dashboard
features.summary: A synced spreadsheet-backed checklist is rendered into searchable tables with local completion tracking.

features.title: Automated rebuild pipeline
features.summary: The site rebuilds on push, manual trigger, and scheduled runs so wiki edits can flow to deployment without manual site editing.

workflows.title: Install and configure
workflows.summary: Start from installation and profile guides, then branch to device-specific notes such as handheld support and save-safe profile usage.

workflows.title: Plan a playthrough
workflows.summary: Use quest and content indices to find level recommendations, mod starts, travel methods, follower fit, and faction-related notes.

workflows.title: Resolve known friction
workflows.summary: Check FAQ and known bug pages first, then use support links when an issue is not already documented.

proofPoints.label: Content footprint
proofPoints.note: Synced from the Tuxborn GitHub Wiki into the local docs set.

proofPoints.label: Update cadence
proofPoints.note: Build workflow is configured for routine refreshes from wiki changes.

proofPoints.label: Source model
proofPoints.note: Contributors keep editing markdown in familiar GitHub wiki flows.

faq.question: Where should edits be made?
faq.answer: Content edits are made in the upstream GitHub Wiki. This site is generated from that source.

faq.question: Is this only for one platform?
faq.answer: No. The docs include Steam Deck and broader handheld guidance plus general PC-focused material.

faq.question: How is checklist progress stored?
faq.answer: Checklist completion uses browser-local storage in the generated static pages.

faq.question: What if a page seems out of date?
faq.answer: Check the upstream wiki first, then trigger a rebuild so the static mirror catches up.

screenshots.alt: Illustrated preview panel representing the Tuxborn Wiki content map
screenshots.caption: Section map preview used for catalog cards and social metadata.

catalog.title: Tuxborn Wiki
catalog.blurb: A maintained navigation layer for installing, understanding, and completing content in the Tuxborn modpack.

repoArchetypeReasoning: README describes a MkDocs site generated from a GitHub Wiki source.
Docs folder contains page-indexed reference content, not application runtime code.
Scripts focus on wiki syncing, link normalization, checklist ingestion, and static deploy workflow.
assumptions: No formal privacy policy URL is present in this repo; privacyUrl remains empty for later central hookup.
Catalog preview art is generated for this export because no dedicated product screenshot asset exists in-repo.
missingButUsefulAssets: Official Tuxborn logo mark with usage-safe dimensions
One or two maintained screenshots of the live documentation experience
Explicit support and privacy page URLs for central site metadata
